title: Secamone schimperiana
taxonomy: Secamone schimperiana belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is a part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants. Within the order Gentianales, the Apocynaceae family is further divided into various subfamilies, with Secamone schimperiana being a part of the subfamily Secamonoideae. Secamone schimperiana is a species of flowering plant native to Madagascar. It is classified as a vine or a climber, and its classification as a species is based on its unique morphological and anatomical characteristics.
uses: Secamone schimperiana has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine for various purposes.
